purpose: compact machine-readable proof for one remote source, one local edited site, and one live drift witness

push_pipeline.preflight: binds the persisted pull base to the live remote identity and a short-lived push session
push_pipeline.snapshot_hash_listing: returns the live remote comparison set for planning only
push_pipeline.dry_run_plan_upload: uploads the canonical plan as eligibility evidence and returns a receipt, not a lock
push_pipeline.mutation_batch_apply: revalidates fresh live evidence before every batch and again at the storage boundary
push_pipeline.journal_inspect: reads durable evidence without authorizing mutation
push_pipeline.recovery: starts with inspect and allows mutating repair only when the journal and live hashes prove the action

apply_revalidation.at_storage_boundary: fresh live hashes plus storage-guard proof
rejected_if: the remote changed after the dry-run receipt
the journal cannot prove a safe finish, rollback, or block

remote_snapshot_hash_listing: cursorable live hash evidence used for planning only and never treated as write authority

roles.remote_base: one remote source site that seeds the persisted pull base
roles.local_edited: one imported local site with user edits
roles.remote_changed: the same remote site observed later after independent drift
roles.runner: the only process allowed to preflight, plan, upload, inspect, revalidate, and recover

pull_pipeline.exporter: scans the merge base and coverage evidence
pull_pipeline.importer: persists the base package as immutable provenance

proof: remote-base and remote-changed are the same remote identity at different times
runner is the only caller that can compare, upload, inspect, and recover
remote-base and remote-changed are the same remote identity at different times
runner uses the same route names and keeps browser-visible inspection on local-only 8080 ingress

required_invariants: dry-run and apply are separate remote operations
remote snapshot hash listing is planning evidence, not write authority
dry-run is a receipt, not a lock
apply must revalidate the live remote before every batch and at the storage boundary
journal inspection is read-only and never authorizes mutation by itself
recovery must begin with inspect before any mutating repair
dry-run and apply are separate remote operations even when the same runner executes both
apply must revalidate fresh live hashes after dry-run and before any storage boundary commit
journal inspect must precede mutating recovery and cannot authorize mutation on its own
mutating recovery must still be fenced by fresh live hashes and journal evidence
